mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decreases in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically raising equity present values and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate above 0.2 percentage points signal rapid labor market deterioration, which reduces household income expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales signal a coordinated collapse in both goods supply and consumer demand, which amplifies downward revisions to corporate revenue and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A linear model with an INDPRO*RSAFS interaction term does not significantly improve pseudo-R-squared over a main-effects-only model for predicting 20-day return direction (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is contracting, a rising unemployment rate confirms a recessionary labor market regime where collapsing wage income depresses aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: recessionary labor market with falling employment
failure_condition: Mean 60-day S&P 500 return is non-negative during months when PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production indicates falling capacity utilization and margin compression across the manufacturing sector, which drags the broad equity index lower and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 60-day S&P 500 forward returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts reflect deteriorating household balance sheets and falling construction activity, which transmit into broad financial market stress and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of weeks following month-over-month HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month accelerations in consumer price inflation above 0.3 percent erode real corporate pricing power and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Mean 20-day S&P 500 return is non-negative during months when the CPIAUCSL trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in industrial production and payroll employment deliver a joint signal of supply-labor market distress that exceeds the sum of individual effects, intensifying deleveraging and producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and PAYEMS changes does not significantly improve the classification accuracy of negative 5-day S&P 500 returns relative to a model without the interaction (delta AUC < 0.02).
